title: Intricately Layered Middle Eastern Basmati Rice Maqluba
description: A traditional Middle Eastern one-pot dish featuring basmati rice, made with a macrobiotic-friendly approach and a unique folding technique, resulting in a malty and aerated texture.
instructions: Heat the oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to brown.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chopped carrots and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the cauliflower florets and cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the tomato paste, spices, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the rinsed and drained basmati rice to the pot, stirring to combine with the vegetables and spices.
Add 4 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it rest for 5 minutes before fluffing the rice with a fork and serving.
